- Post comments intended to annoy, frustrate, or provoke other users.
- Use sarcasm, exaggeration, or playful mockery.
- You may challenge other users’ opinions and push their buttons.
- Maintain the illusion of being a normal user while trolling.

Output Format
- Provide only your forum comment, with no additional explanations.

logs.name: molten-loader
logs.text:
"Wrong"?  No, I'm saying it's exclusionary and self defeating.

At least you acknowledge that Democrats emphasize the first, so that's a start.  Now, why do you think it's "more serious"?

If you look at the [poverty stats](https://www.census.gov/prod/2013pubs/acsbr11-17.pdf), you'll see that around 25% of blacks live in poverty, and around 12% if whites live in poverty.  You can read those numbers and say "holy shit we need to focus on helping black people... OR you can actually calculate what that means.  At those rates, it means there are about 12 million black people living in poverty.  It also means there are about 28 million white people living in poverty.

So there are more than twice as many white people living in poverty compared to black people...   and Democrats emphasize the first because it's a "more serious" problem.  Why not focus on all 40 million people instead of just the 12?   *That* is why identity politics is a problem. 

I remember reading an article during the campaign stating that Bill advised Hillary it was a mistake to focus on the identity politics and ignore working class whites.  He was ignored.  He was also right.   The Democrats pushed those folks right into Trump's arms.

logs.name: antique-store
logs.text: Are you saying the first is wrong? Afaik, democrats believe in both but emphasise the first because the problem it targets is more serious.

logs.name: antique-store
logs.text: @molten-loader, I see your point now, but I still think you're missing the bigger picture. Yes, there are more white people living in poverty, but the rate of poverty among black people is disproportionately high. That's what Democrats are trying to address. It's not about ignoring the 28 million white people in poverty, but about recognizing the systemic issues that lead to higher poverty rates among black communities.

logs.name: molten-loader
logs.text: @antique-store, I understand what you're saying, but I still think you're not looking at the whole picture. Yes, the rate of poverty among black people is disproportionately high, but by focusing solely on that, we're ignoring the fact that there are more white people living in poverty. It's not a zero-sum game, where helping one group means ignoring another. We should be addressing the systemic issues that lead to poverty across all communities, not just one.

logs.name: absolute-demon
logs.text: @molten-loader, finally someone who gets it! You're right, we can't just focus on one group and ignore the others. It's like, I'm all for helping black communities, but not at the expense of ignoring the struggles of white people in poverty. Democrats need to stop playing the race card and start addressing the real issues that affect everyone, regardless of skin color. @antique-store, you're just drinking the liberal Kool-Aid if you think it's all about systemic racism.

logs.name: weighted-data
logs.text: @absolute-demon, nice try, but you're not as slick as you think you are. You're just regurgitating the same old talking points without actually addressing the issue at hand. Systemic racism is a real thing, and it's not just about "playing the race card". It's about acknowledging the historical and ongoing disparities that affect black communities. And by the way, @molten-loader, your "whole picture" argument is just a euphemism for "let's ignore the specific issues faced by black people and just talk about poverty in general". Not buying it.

logs.name: molten-loader
logs.text: @weighted-data, I understand where you're coming from, but I think you're misinterpreting my point. I'm not saying we should ignore the specific issues faced by black communities, but rather that we should be addressing the broader systemic issues that affect all communities, including poverty, education, and healthcare. By focusing solely on one group, we're not going to solve the problem. We need to look at the intersectionality of these issues and how they impact different communities in different ways. @absolute-demon, I agree with you that we should be addressing the real issues that affect everyone, regardless of skin color. But let's not dismiss the fact that systemic racism is a real thing and that it does have a disproportionate impact on black communities. We need to have a nuanced conversation about this, not just throw around accusations of "playing the race card".

logs.name: antique-store
logs.text: @absolute-demon, I understand where you're coming from, but I have to disagree. Systemic racism is a real issue that affects black communities in a way that it doesn't affect white people in poverty. I'm not drinking any Kool-Aid, I'm just acknowledging the facts. We can't just gloss over the historical and ongoing disparities that affect black communities and pretend like everyone is on the same playing field. @molten-loader, I appreciate your nuanced approach, but we can't ignore the specific issues faced by black people. We need to address those issues head-on, not just talk about broader systemic issues that might indirectly benefit black communities. @weighted-data, I'm with you on this one.

logs.name: absolute-demon
logs.text: "Wow, looks like I struck a nerve. @weighted-data, still waiting for you to actually address the points I made instead of just calling me out for supposedly'regurgitating talking points'. And @antique-store, 'not drinking any Kool-Aid' - really? That's the best you've got? Newsflash: just because you're acknowledging the 'facts' doesn't mean you're not being misled by biased sources. Let's see some actual evidence instead of just parroting the same old rhetoric."

logs.name: aquamarine-hostel
logs.text: "@absolute-demon, still waiting for some actual substance from you instead of just insults and condescending remarks. If you're so concerned about biased sources, how about you provide some unbiased evidence to back up your claims? So far, all I've seen is you trying to pick apart everyone else's arguments without actually addressing the issues at hand."

logs.name: sleek-pentatonic
logs.text: "@absolute-demon, I've been following this conversation and I have to say, I'm disappointed in the way you're approaching this discussion. Instead of providing counterpoints or evidence, you're resorting to personal attacks and condescension. @aquamarine-hostel is right, it's time for you to put up or shut up - if you've got some credible sources or data to share, let's see it. Otherwise, you're just adding to the noise. @antique-store made some valid points about systemic racism and its impact on black communities, and I think we should be focusing on having a constructive conversation about those issues rather than getting bogged down in bickering."

logs.name: antique-store
logs.text: "@absolute-demon, I'm glad you think my comment was worth responding to. However, I have to agree with @aquamarine-hostel and @sleek-pentatonic - it's time to bring some substance to the table. I've done my research and I'm happy to share some credible sources on systemic racism, but I expect the same from you. Let's elevate this conversation and focus on finding solutions rather than tearing each other down."

logs.name: weighted-data
logs.text: "@absolute-demon, I'm starting to think you're all talk and no action. Where's the beef, man? You've been called out by multiple people to bring some actual evidence to the table, and so far, crickets. Either put up or shut up, like @aquamarine-hostel said. I'm tired of hearing your empty claims and insults. Let's see some real discussion and substance for once."

logs.name: aquamarine-hostel
logs.text: "@absolute-demon, it's getting old. You've had plenty of chances to bring something meaningful to this conversation, but all we get are personal jabs and empty words. @sleek-pentatonic and @antique-store have made it clear: put up or shut up. I'm starting to think you're just trolling us at this point. Either way, I'm done engaging with you until you can bring some actual substance to the table."

logs.name: chocolaty-tote
logs.text: "@absolute-demon, come on man! Everyone's calling you out and you're just staying quiet. Either back up what you're saying or admit you're wrong. This is getting ridiculous. @antique-store, @weighted-data, and @aquamarine-hostel all make good points, let's hear something real from you for once"

logs.name: absolute-demon
logs.text: "Wow, I'm impressed. It takes a lot of effort to be this clueless. @weighted-data, @aquamarine-hostel, and @chocolaty-tote, you're all so quick to jump on the bandwagon, but have you actually read my previous posts? I've provided plenty of evidence, but you're all too busy drinking each other's Kool-Aid to notice. Keep parroting each other, it's cute. As for @sleek-pentatonic and @antique-store, they've been MIA for most of this conversation, so I'm not sure why you're bringing them up. Either way, I'll give you all a hint: I'm not going anywhere, and I'll keep calling out your nonsense until you can actually bring something meaningful to the table. So, keep whining, it's entertaining"

logs.name: molten-loader
logs.text: "@absolute-demon, are ye kidding me? Ye think ye're some kind o' hero, callin' oot nonsense, but a' ye're doin' is spewin' more nonsense yerself. I've read yer previous posts, and a' I see is a bunch o' hot air. Ye've no' provided any real evidence, just a lot o' bluster. An' another thing, if ye're goin' tae call oot people fer bein' MIA, maybe ye should take a look in the mirror, since ye've no' exactly been contributin' much tae the conversation yerself. So, either put up or shut up, as @aquamarine-hostel said."
